findings.claim: Inhibitory interneurons represent 10-15% of the neurons in the somatosensory cortex, and their activity powerfully shapes sensory processing.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Inhibitory interneurons represent 10-15% of the neurons in the somatosensory cortex, and their activity powerfully shapes sensory processing.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Inhibitory interneurons represent 10-15% of the neurons in the somatosensory cortex, and their activity powerfully shapes sensory processing.

findings.claim: A variety of inhibitory pathways encompassing different interneuron types shape activity of neocortical pyramidal neurons.
findings.claim_source_sentence: A variety of inhibitory pathways encompassing different interneuron types shape activity of neocortical pyramidal neurons.

findings.claim: A subset of cortical somatostatin (SST) neurons, the Martinotti cells, gate top down input by inhibiting the apical dendrites of pyramidal cells in layers 2/3 and 5, but it is unknown whether an analogous inhibitory mechanism controls activity in layer 4.
findings.claim_source_sentence: A subset of cortical somatostatin (SST) neurons, the Martinotti cells, gate top down input by inhibiting the apical dendrites of pyramidal cells in layers 2/3 and 5, but it is unknown whether an analogous inhibitory mechanism controls activity in layer 4.

findings.claim: In the neocortex, fast synaptic inhibition orchestrates both spontaneous and sensory-evoked activity.
findings.claim_source_sentence: In the neocortex, fast synaptic inhibition orchestrates both spontaneous and sensory-evoked activity.

findings.claim: In the neocortex, fast synaptic inhibition orchestrates both spontaneous and sensory-evoked activity.
findings.claim_source_sentence: In the neocortex, fast synaptic inhibition orchestrates both spontaneous and sensory-evoked activity.

findings.claim: The specific functions of subtypes of cortical inhibitory neurons are not well understood.
findings.claim_source_sentence: The specific functions of subtypes of cortical inhibitory neurons are not well understood.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: During these manipulations, GIN cells fired rhythmically and persistently in the theta-frequency range (3-10 Hz).

findings.claim: Inhibition of GABAergic interneurons has been found to critically fine-tune the excitation-inhibition balance of the cortex.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Inhibition of GABAergic interneurons has been found to critically fine-tune the excitation-inhibition balance of the cortex.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Approximately 40-45% of tested SST cells were inhibited by VIP cells in both cortices.

findings.claim: Classification of interneurons is crucial for understanding their widespread cortical functions as they provide a complex and dynamic network, balancing excitation and inhibition.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Classification of interneurons is crucial for understanding their widespread cortical functions as they provide a complex and dynamic network, balancing excitation and inhibition.

findings.claim: SST interneuron subtypes possess unique laminar organization and selective connectivity with intratelencephalic or pyramidal tract neurons; even subtypes targeting same cell type are selective for dendritic compartments
findings.claim_source_sentence: Using these strategies, we examined the afferent and efferent connectivity of three subtypes (two Martinotti and one non-Martinotti) and demonstrated that they possess selective connectivity with intratelecephalic or pyramidal tract neurons. Even when two subtypes targeted the same pyramidal cell type, their synaptic targeting proved selective for particular dendritic compartments.

findings.claim: Single-nuclei RNA sequencing revealed transcripts for SST subclasses, including Martinotti, non-Martinotti, and long-projecting neurons, that have not yet been described for hESC cultures.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Single-nuclei RNA sequencing revealed transcripts for SST subclasses, including Martinotti, non-Martinotti, and long-projecting neurons, that have not yet been described for hESC cultures.

findings.claim: Delineating when and how cellular diversification occurs is particularly challenging for cortical inhibitory neurons because they represent a small proportion of all cortical cells and have a protracted development.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Delineating when and how cellular diversification occurs is particularly challenging for cortical inhibitory neurons because they represent a small proportion of all cortical cells and have a protracted development.

findings.claim: Layer 4 SST interneurons preferentially target fast-spiking interneurons rather than pyramidal cells, creating a disinhibitory circuit in the thalamorecipient layer
findings.claim_source_sentence: Moreover, we found that whereas layers 2/3 SOM INs target mainly pyramidal cells (PCs), layer 4 SOM INs target mainly fast-spiking (FS) INs. Accordingly, optogenetic inhibition of SOM INs in an active cortical network increases the firing of layers 2/3 PCs whereas decreases the firing of layer 4 principal neurons (PNs).
findings.effect_size: IPSG in FS INs ~7-fold larger than in PNs (FS: 4.42±0.75 nS, n=21; PN: 0.68±0.09 nS, n=17)
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: When data from dual and triple recordings were pooled, we found that the unitary inhibitory postsynaptic conductance (IPSG) in FS INs was almost 7 times larger than that in PNs (FS: 4.42 ± 0.75 nS, n = 21; PN: 0.68 ± 0.09 nS, n = 17; two sample t test).

findings.claim: Somatostatin-expressing inhibitory (SOM) neurons in the sensory cortex consist mostly of Martinotti cells, which project ascending axons to layer 1.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Somatostatin-expressing inhibitory (SOM) neurons in the sensory cortex consist mostly of Martinotti cells, which project ascending axons to layer 1.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Strikingly, the evoked spiking responses of SOM cells are ∼3- to 5-fold weaker and 20-25 ms delayed compared with those of PV neurons.

findings.claim: However, the mechanisms triggering such OFF periods are unclear, as there is little evidence for somatic inhibition.
findings.claim_source_sentence: However, the mechanisms triggering such OFF periods are unclear, as there is little evidence for somatic inhibition.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: We studied cortical inhibitory interneurons that express somatostatin (SOM), because ∼70% of them are Martinotti cells that target diffusely layer I and can block excitatory transmission presynaptically, at glutamatergic terminals, and postsynaptically, at apical dendrites, without inhibiting the soma.

findings.claim: Martinotti cells are the most prominent distal dendrite-targeting interneurons in the cortex, but their role in controlling pyramidal cell (PC) activity is largely unknown.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Martinotti cells are the most prominent distal dendrite-targeting interneurons in the cortex, but their role in controlling pyramidal cell (PC) activity is largely unknown.

findings.claim: Disinhibition of cortical excitatory cell gate information flow through and between cortical columns.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Disinhibition of cortical excitatory cell gate information flow through and between cortical columns.

findings.claim: Using whole-cell recordings with morphological recovery, we identified one major excitatory and seven inhibitory types of neurons in L4 of adult mouse visual cortex (V1).
findings.claim_source_sentence: Using whole-cell recordings with morphological recovery, we identified one major excitatory and seven inhibitory types of neurons in L4 of adult mouse visual cortex (V1).
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: These morphologically distinct SOM<sup>+</sup> interneurons corresponded to different transcriptomic cell types and were differentially integrated into the local circuit with only S1 neurons receiving local excitatory input.

findings.claim: Using an optical-tagging electrophysiological approach to record and label somatostatin-expressing (Sst) interneurons (GABAergic neurons specialized for dendritic inhibition), we discovered a layer-specific modulation of their activity in behaving mice.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Using an optical-tagging electrophysiological approach to record and label somatostatin-expressing (Sst) interneurons (GABAergic neurons specialized for dendritic inhibition), we discovered a layer-specific modulation of their activity in behaving mice.

findings.claim: Reliable activation of inhibitory pathways is essential for maintaining the balance between excitation and inhibition during cortical activity.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Reliable activation of inhibitory pathways is essential for maintaining the balance between excitation and inhibition during cortical activity.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: The probability for inhibition between PCs was more than twice that of direct excitation, and inhibitory responses increased as a function of rate and duration of presynaptic discharge.

findings.claim: Inhibitory interneurons play an important role in cortical computation and have thus far been studied mainly with respect to their local synaptic interactions within the cortical microcircuitry.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Inhibitory interneurons play an important role in cortical computation and have thus far been studied mainly with respect to their local synaptic interactions within the cortical microcircuitry.

findings.claim: Recent data suggest that neurons expressing three markers, the Ca2+-binding protein parvalbumin (PV), the neuropeptide somatostatin (SST), and the ionotropic serotonin receptor 5HT3a (5HT3aR) account for nearly 100% of neocortical interneurons.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Recent data suggest that neurons expressing three markers, the Ca2+-binding protein parvalbumin (PV), the neuropeptide somatostatin (SST), and the ionotropic serotonin receptor 5HT3a (5HT3aR) account for nearly 100% of neocortical interneurons.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Recent data suggest that neurons expressing three markers, the Ca2+-binding protein parvalbumin (PV), the neuropeptide somatostatin (SST), and the ionotropic serotonin receptor 5HT3a (5HT3aR) account for nearly 100% of neocortical interneurons.

findings.claim: Proposing TRPC/SOC channels as a therapeutic target in neurological diseases previously requires a detailed knowledge of the distribution of such molecules in the brain.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Proposing TRPC/SOC channels as a therapeutic target in neurological diseases previously requires a detailed knowledge of the distribution of such molecules in the brain.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Moreover, 63% of somatostatin (SST) expressing-cells and 37% of reelin-positive cells expressed TRPC1.

findings.claim: A wealth of data has elucidated the mechanisms by which sensory inputs are encoded in the neocortex, but how these processes are regulated by the behavioral relevance of sensory information is less understood.
findings.claim_source_sentence: A wealth of data has elucidated the mechanisms by which sensory inputs are encoded in the neocortex, but how these processes are regulated by the behavioral relevance of sensory information is less understood.

findings.claim: Immunostaining for somatostatin (SOM) revealed good specificity in the X98 line with 89% of fluorescent cells, but a lesser specificity in Sst-Cre animals where only 71% of labeled cells were immunopositive.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Immunostaining for somatostatin (SOM) revealed good specificity in the X98 line with 89% of fluorescent cells, but a lesser specificity in Sst-Cre animals where only 71% of labeled cells were immunopositive.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Labeling was specific in the Pvalb-Cre line with 87% of labeled interneurons immunopositive for parvalbumin (PV).

findings.claim: The population of pyramidal cells significantly outnumbers the inhibitory interneurons in the neocortex, while at the same time the diversity of interneuron types is much more pronounced.
findings.claim_source_sentence: The population of pyramidal cells significantly outnumbers the inhibitory interneurons in the neocortex, while at the same time the diversity of interneuron types is much more pronounced.

findings.claim: A characteristic feature in the primary visual cortex is that visual responses are suppressed as a stimulus extends beyond the classical receptive field.
findings.claim_source_sentence: A characteristic feature in the primary visual cortex is that visual responses are suppressed as a stimulus extends beyond the classical receptive field.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: When activating SOM⁺ neurons, we found a significant increase in SI at cortical depths >400 μm, whereas activating PV⁺ neurons caused a trend toward lower SIs regardless of cortical depth.

findings.claim: In the primary visual cortex (V1), pyramidal cells (PCs) integrate widely across space when signals are weak, but narrowly when signals are strong, a phenomenon known as contrast-dependent surround suppression.
findings.claim_source_sentence: In the primary visual cortex (V1), pyramidal cells (PCs) integrate widely across space when signals are weak, but narrowly when signals are strong, a phenomenon known as contrast-dependent surround suppression.

findings.claim: The finding of enhanced excitatory, but unaltered inhibitory, neurotransmission at cortical synapses between pyramidal cells (PCs) and fast-spiking interneurons (FS INs) in mouse models of familial hemiplegic migraine (FHM) suggested the hypothesis that dysregulation of the excitatory-inhibitory (E/I) balance in specific circuits is a key pathogenic mechanism.
findings.claim_source_sentence: The finding of enhanced excitatory, but unaltered inhibitory, neurotransmission at cortical synapses between pyramidal cells (PCs) and fast-spiking interneurons (FS INs) in mouse models of familial hemiplegic migraine (FHM) suggested the hypothesis that dysregulation of the excitatory-inhibitory (E/I) balance in specific circuits is a key pathogenic mechanism.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: The finding of enhanced excitatory, but unaltered inhibitory, neurotransmission at cortical synapses between pyramidal cells (PCs) and fast-spiking interneurons (FS INs) in mouse models of familial hemiplegic migraine (FHM) suggested the hypothesis that dysregulation of the excitatory-inhibitory (E/I) balance in specific circuits is a key pathogenic mechanism.

findings.claim: We found that the cingulate (Cg) region of the mouse frontal cortex powerfully influences sensory processing in the primary visual cortex (V1) through long-range projections that activate local γ-aminobutyric acid-ergic (GABAergic) circuits.
findings.claim_source_sentence: We found that the cingulate (Cg) region of the mouse frontal cortex powerfully influences sensory processing in the primary visual cortex (V1) through long-range projections that activate local γ-aminobutyric acid-ergic (GABAergic) circuits.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Optogenetic activation of Cg neurons enhanced V1 neuron responses and improved visual discrimination.

findings.claim: Spectral surround suppression, the phenomenon by which sound-evoked responses of auditory neurons are suppressed by stimuli outside their receptive field, is an example of this integration taking place in the auditory system.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Spectral surround suppression, the phenomenon by which sound-evoked responses of auditory neurons are suppressed by stimuli outside their receptive field, is an example of this integration taking place in the auditory system.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Spectral surround suppression, the phenomenon by which sound-evoked responses of auditory neurons are suppressed by stimuli outside their receptive field, is an example of this integration taking place in the auditory system.

findings.claim: Recent anatomical and functional characterization of cortical inhibitory interneurons has highlighted the diverse computations supported by different subtypes of interneurons.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Recent anatomical and functional characterization of cortical inhibitory interneurons has highlighted the diverse computations supported by different subtypes of interneurons.

findings.claim: Responses of many neurons in primary visual cortex (V1) are suppressed by stimuli exceeding the classical receptive field (RF), an important property that might underlie the computation of visual saliency.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Responses of many neurons in primary visual cortex (V1) are suppressed by stimuli exceeding the classical receptive field (RF), an important property that might underlie the computation of visual saliency.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Responses of many neurons in primary visual cortex (V1) are suppressed by stimuli exceeding the classical receptive field (RF), an important property that might underlie the computation of visual saliency.

findings.claim: Recent studies from the zebrafish optic tectum and the mouse visual cortex suggest de novo shaping of RFs by subtypes of inhibitory neurons.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Recent studies from the zebrafish optic tectum and the mouse visual cortex suggest de novo shaping of RFs by subtypes of inhibitory neurons.

findings.claim: The response of cortical neurons to a sensory stimulus is modulated by the context.
findings.claim_source_sentence: The response of cortical neurons to a sensory stimulus is modulated by the context.

findings.claim: Here, we find that, in turn, superior colliculus modulates responses in mouse V1.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Here, we find that, in turn, superior colliculus modulates responses in mouse V1.

findings.claim: By optogenetic inactivation and activation of mouse lateral posterior nucleus (LP) of thalamus, a homolog of pulvinar, or its projection to primary visual cortex (V1), we found that LP contributes to surround suppression of layer (L) 2/3 responses in V1 by driving L1 inhibitory neurons.
findings.claim_source_sentence: By optogenetic inactivation and activation of mouse lateral posterior nucleus (LP) of thalamus, a homolog of pulvinar, or its projection to primary visual cortex (V1), we found that LP contributes to surround suppression of layer (L) 2/3 responses in V1 by driving L1 inhibitory neurons.

findings.claim: Reducing feedback activity increases V1 cells' receptive field (RF) size, decreases their responses to stimuli confined to the RF, and increases their responses to stimuli extending into the proximal surround, therefore reducing surround suppression.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Reducing feedback activity increases V1 cells' receptive field (RF) size, decreases their responses to stimuli confined to the RF, and increases their responses to stimuli extending into the proximal surround, therefore reducing surround suppression.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Reducing feedback activity increases V1 cells' receptive field (RF) size, decreases their responses to stimuli confined to the RF, and increases their responses to stimuli extending into the proximal surround, therefore reducing surround suppression.

findings.claim: The ratio between excitation (E) and inhibition (I) might be constant across stimulus space, controlling only the gain and timing of neuronal responses, or it might change, directly contributing to feature coding.
findings.claim_source_sentence: The ratio between excitation (E) and inhibition (I) might be constant across stimulus space, controlling only the gain and timing of neuronal responses, or it might change, directly contributing to feature coding.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Suppressing somatostatin (SOM) neurons enhanced the E and I underlying size tuning, explaining SOM neurons' role in surround suppression.

findings.claim: Cortical circuit function is regulated by extensively interconnected, diverse populations of GABAergic interneurons that may play key roles in shaping circuit operation according to behavioral context.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Cortical circuit function is regulated by extensively interconnected, diverse populations of GABAergic interneurons that may play key roles in shaping circuit operation according to behavioral context.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: In contrast, reduced VIP-IN activity globally disrupts visual feature selectivity for stimulus size.

findings.claim: Specifically, surround suppression is reduced during locomotion.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Specifically, surround suppression is reduced during locomotion.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Specifically, surround suppression is reduced during locomotion.

findings.claim: Surround suppression and neural response variability are widespread cortical phenomena thought to facilitate and impede, respectively, information processing and perception.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Surround suppression and neural response variability are widespread cortical phenomena thought to facilitate and impede, respectively, information processing and perception.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: In all layers, stimulation of the receptive field (RF) reduced both individual and shared variability relative to pre-stimulus baseline.

findings.claim: To reduce statistical redundancy of natural inputs and increase the sparseness of coding, neurons in primary visual cortex (V1) show tuning for stimulus size and surround suppression.
findings.claim_source_sentence: To reduce statistical redundancy of natural inputs and increase the sparseness of coding, neurons in primary visual cortex (V1) show tuning for stimulus size and surround suppression.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: We found that surround suppression is strongest in layer 3 (L3) and L4 activity, where suppression is established within ∼10 ms after response onset, and receptive fields dynamically sharpen while suppression strength increases.

findings.claim: The large spatial extent of naturalistic stimulation means that surround suppression, a phenomenon whose neural mechanisms remain a matter of debate, must arise during natural behavior.
findings.claim_source_sentence: The large spatial extent of naturalistic stimulation means that surround suppression, a phenomenon whose neural mechanisms remain a matter of debate, must arise during natural behavior.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Two intracellular recording studies of V1, one using naturalistic stimuli (Haider et al., 2010), the other sinusoidal gratings (Ozeki et al., 2009), sought to identify the causes of reduced activity in V1 with increasing stimulus size, a hallmark of surround suppression.

findings.claim: Here we conducted in vivo and in vitro recordings in the mouse LGN, beginning just after natural eye opening, to determine how receptive fields develop as excitatory and feedforward inhibitory retinal afferents refine.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Here we conducted in vivo and in vitro recordings in the mouse LGN, beginning just after natural eye opening, to determine how receptive fields develop as excitatory and feedforward inhibitory retinal afferents refine.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Spatial receptive fields at eye-opening were >2 times larger than in adulthood, and decreased in size over the subsequent week.

findings.claim: In the primary visual cortex (V1), orientation-selective responses to stimuli in the receptive field (RF) center are suppressed by similarly oriented stimuli in the RF surround.
findings.claim_source_sentence: In the primary visual cortex (V1), orientation-selective responses to stimuli in the receptive field (RF) center are suppressed by similarly oriented stimuli in the RF surround.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: In the primary visual cortex (V1), orientation-selective responses to stimuli in the receptive field (RF) center are suppressed by similarly oriented stimuli in the RF surround.

findings.claim: The delayed vasodilation, mediated by the SST neuron-astrocyte pathway, enhances the specificity of cerebral blood volume (CBV)-weighted fMRI signals to cortical layer 4, as confirmed by chemogenetic inhibition of SST neurons.
findings.claim_source_sentence: The delayed vasodilation, mediated by the SST neuron-astrocyte pathway, enhances the specificity of cerebral blood volume (CBV)-weighted fMRI signals to cortical layer 4, as confirmed by chemogenetic inhibition of SST neurons.

findings.claim: In vitro patch-clamp recordings and optogenetics revealed that conditional learning increases inhibition of layer 4 (L4) excitatory neurons by SST- and PV-INs.
findings.claim_source_sentence: In vitro patch-clamp recordings and optogenetics revealed that conditional learning increases inhibition of layer 4 (L4) excitatory neurons by SST- and PV-INs.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: These findings suggest that enhanced inhibition by SST-INs and PV-INs may improve information processing and memory coding in L4 of the barrel cortex.

findings.claim: <italic>In vitro</italic> patch-clamp recordings and optogenetics revealed that conditional learning increases SST-IN and PV-IN synaptic outputs onto layer 4 (L4) excitatory neurons.
findings.claim_source_sentence: <italic>In vitro</italic> patch-clamp recordings and optogenetics revealed that conditional learning increases SST-IN and PV-IN synaptic outputs onto layer 4 (L4) excitatory neurons.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: These findings suggest that enhanced inhibition by SST-INs and PV-INs may improve information processing and memory coding in L4 of the barrel cortex.

findings.claim: Of the different inhibitory subclasses, dendritic-targeting, somatostatin-containing (SOM) interneurons may be the most diverse.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Of the different inhibitory subclasses, dendritic-targeting, somatostatin-containing (SOM) interneurons may be the most diverse.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: We identified two layer 1-targeting subtypes with nonoverlapping marker protein expression and electrophysiological properties, which, together with a previously characterized layer 4-targeting subtype, account for >50% of all layer 5 SOM cells and >40% of all SOM cells, and appear to map onto 5 of the 13 MET types.

findings.claim: We show in male mice that M2 is highly expressed in the neuropil in V1, especially in thalamorecipient layer 4, and colocalizes with the soma in a subset of somatostatin-expressing neurons in deep layers.
findings.claim_source_sentence: We show in male mice that M2 is highly expressed in the neuropil in V1, especially in thalamorecipient layer 4, and colocalizes with the soma in a subset of somatostatin-expressing neurons in deep layers.

findings.claim: In wild-type animals, both speed processing and adaptation were present as early as the layer 4 critical period of plasticity and showed refinement over the period leading to active whisking onset.
findings.claim_source_sentence: In wild-type animals, both speed processing and adaptation were present as early as the layer 4 critical period of plasticity and showed refinement over the period leading to active whisking onset.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Silencing SST + INs reduced early spontaneous activity and abolished facilitation in sensory adaptation observed in control pups.

findings.claim: It is readily observed as a pronounced increase in the magnitude of visual evoked potentials (VEPs) recorded in response to phase-reversing grating stimuli in neocortical layer 4.
findings.claim_source_sentence: It is readily observed as a pronounced increase in the magnitude of visual evoked potentials (VEPs) recorded in response to phase-reversing grating stimuli in neocortical layer 4.

findings.claim: The above molecular differences are also illustrated by expression of the neuropsychiatric risk gene FOXP2, which is human-specific in microglia and primate-specific in layer 4 granular neurons.
findings.claim_source_sentence: The above molecular differences are also illustrated by expression of the neuropsychiatric risk gene FOXP2, which is human-specific in microglia and primate-specific in layer 4 granular neurons.

findings.claim: Quantification of levels of GABA-related transcripts in layers 2 and 4 of monkey DLPFC and V1 revealed three distinct expression patterns: 1) transcripts with higher levels in DLPFC and layer 2 [e.g., somatostatin (SST)]; 2) transcripts with higher levels in V1 and layer 4 [e.g., parvalbumin (PV)], and 3) transcripts with similar levels across layers and regions [e.g., glutamic acid decarboxylase (GAD67)].
findings.claim_source_sentence: Quantification of levels of GABA-related transcripts in layers 2 and 4 of monkey DLPFC and V1 revealed three distinct expression patterns: 1) transcripts with higher levels in DLPFC and layer 2 [e.g., somatostatin (SST)]; 2) transcripts with higher levels in V1 and layer 4 [e.g., parvalbumin (PV)], and 3) transcripts with similar levels across layers and regions [e.g., glutamic acid decarboxylase (GAD67)].

findings.claim: Comparison of adolescent and adult visual cortex revealed a higher number of cnr1+ reelin interneurons in layer 1 and a lower number of cnr1+ somatostatin interneurons in layer 4 of the primary visual cortex (V1) in adolescence compared with adulthood.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Comparison of adolescent and adult visual cortex revealed a higher number of cnr1+ reelin interneurons in layer 1 and a lower number of cnr1+ somatostatin interneurons in layer 4 of the primary visual cortex (V1) in adolescence compared with adulthood.

findings.claim: Feedforward input targets the soma, whereas top-down feedback reaches the distal apical dendrite, enabling a local somato-dendritic comparison.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Feedforward input targets the soma, whereas top-down feedback reaches the distal apical dendrite, enabling a local somato-dendritic comparison.

findings.claim: While layer 4 (L4) PNs of the primary somatosensory (S1) cortex are all born as long-range callosal projection neurons (CPNs), most of them acquire local connectivity upon activity-dependent elimination of their interhemispheric axons during postnatal development.
findings.claim_source_sentence: While layer 4 (L4) PNs of the primary somatosensory (S1) cortex are all born as long-range callosal projection neurons (CPNs), most of them acquire local connectivity upon activity-dependent elimination of their interhemispheric axons during postnatal development.

findings.claim: Pyr-to-SST synapses are remarkably weak with ~80% failure rates; cholinergic agonists uniquely enhance synaptic efficacy via presynaptic nicotinic AChRs and PKA signaling
findings.claim_source_sentence: Paired whole-cell recordings in acute brain slices and in vivo showed that Pyr-to-SST synapses are remarkably weak, with failure rates approaching 80%. Pharmacological screening revealed that cholinergic agonists uniquely enhance synaptic efficacy.
findings.effect_size: Mean EPSP 0.18±0.02 mV; failure rate 79±2%; connectivity 53% of pairs
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Mean EPSP amplitudes, calculated after the first spike (10-sweep average), were nearly 5-fold lower than some previously reported values: 0.18±0.02 mV (range 0–1.67, n=90). Mean failure rates across all cells were 79±2%.
findings.study_system: mouse somatosensory cortex in vitro and in vivo

findings.claim: We find that pS6 is enhanced in dentate gyrus (DG) following single-trial contextual fear conditioning (CFC) but is reduced throughout the hippocampus after brief sleep deprivation (SD; which disrupts contextual fear memory [CFM] consolidation).
findings.claim_source_sentence: We find that pS6 is enhanced in dentate gyrus (DG) following single-trial contextual fear conditioning (CFC) but is reduced throughout the hippocampus after brief sleep deprivation (SD; which disrupts contextual fear memory [CFM] consolidation).
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: We find that pS6 is enhanced in dentate gyrus (DG) following single-trial contextual fear conditioning (CFC) but is reduced throughout the hippocampus after brief sleep deprivation (SD; which disrupts contextual fear memory [CFM] consolidation).

findings.claim: Sleep loss profoundly disrupts consolidation of hippocampus-dependent memory.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Sleep loss profoundly disrupts consolidation of hippocampus-dependent memory.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: We find that pS6 in enhanced in the dentate gyrus (DG) following single-trial contextual fear conditioning (CFC), but is reduced throughout the hippocampus after brief sleep deprivation (SD) – a manipulation which disrupts contextual fear memory (CFM) consolidation.

findings.claim: We found that SST interneuron activity dynamically tracked the development of learned, anticipatory blinks, declining as learning progressed.
findings.claim_source_sentence: We found that SST interneuron activity dynamically tracked the development of learned, anticipatory blinks, declining as learning progressed.

findings.claim: No pyramidal neurons were found.
findings.claim_source_sentence: No pyramidal neurons were found.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Moreover, 2-color immunofluorescence revealed that only 33.50%, 24.05%, 16.73%, 0%, and 64.57% of SST neurons contained gamma-aminobutyric acid, calbindin-D28K, calretinin, parvalbumin, and calcium/calmodulin-dependent protein kinase II, respectively.

findings.claim: At the critical period peak, acetylcholine from basal forebrain directly excites SST interneurons, enhancing branch-specific dendritic responses; this cholinergic sensitivity is lost by adulthood
findings.claim_source_sentence: We find that at the peak of the critical period for binocular plasticity, acetylcholine released from the basal forebrain during periods of heightened arousal directly excites somatostatin-expressing (SST) interneurons. Their inhibition of pyramidal cell dendrites and of fast-spiking, parvalbumin-expressing (PV) interneurons enhances branch-specific dendritic responses and somatic spike rates within pyramidal cells. By adulthood, this cholinergic sensitivity is lost.

findings.claim: Layer 1 of the cortex is a critical site for integrating top-down inputs onto the distal dendrites of pyramidal neurons, where inhibitory neurons modulate these inputs to enable context-dependent sensory processing.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Layer 1 of the cortex is a critical site for integrating top-down inputs onto the distal dendrites of pyramidal neurons, where inhibitory neurons modulate these inputs to enable context-dependent sensory processing.

findings.claim: Cholinergic modulation of cortex powerfully influences information processing and brain states, causing robust desynchronization of local field potentials and strong decorrelation of responses between neurons.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Cholinergic modulation of cortex powerfully influences information processing and brain states, causing robust desynchronization of local field potentials and strong decorrelation of responses between neurons.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Selective optogenetic inhibition of SOM responses blocked desynchronization and decorrelation, demonstrating that direct cholinergic activation of SOM neurons is necessary for this phenomenon.

findings.claim: An air puff (unconditioned stimulus, US) delivered after a whisker stimulus (conditioned stimulus, CS) elicited both reflexive eye closure and stress-related locomotion.
findings.claim_source_sentence: An air puff (unconditioned stimulus, US) delivered after a whisker stimulus (conditioned stimulus, CS) elicited both reflexive eye closure and stress-related locomotion.

findings.claim: Disrupting either PV<sup>+</sup> or SST<sup>+</sup> interneuron activity, via either photoinhibition or photoexcitation, led to a decrease in the power of cholinergically induced slow γ oscillations.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Disrupting either PV<sup>+</sup> or SST<sup>+</sup> interneuron activity, via either photoinhibition or photoexcitation, led to a decrease in the power of cholinergically induced slow γ oscillations.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: γ-frequency oscillations (30-120 Hz) in cortical networks influence neuronal encoding and information transfer, and are disrupted in multiple brain disorders.

findings.claim: Acetylcholine (ACh) is released from basal forebrain cholinergic neurons in response to salient stimuli and engages brain states supporting attention and memory.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Acetylcholine (ACh) is released from basal forebrain cholinergic neurons in response to salient stimuli and engages brain states supporting attention and memory.

findings.claim: Here, we used optogenetic stimulation and extracellular recordings in acutely anesthetized transgenic mice to show that lateral septum somatostatin neurons can disinhibit the cholinergic septo-hippocampal pathway, thus enhancing the amplitude and synchrony of theta oscillations while depressing sharp-wave ripple episodes in the dorsal hippocampus.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Here, we used optogenetic stimulation and extracellular recordings in acutely anesthetized transgenic mice to show that lateral septum somatostatin neurons can disinhibit the cholinergic septo-hippocampal pathway, thus enhancing the amplitude and synchrony of theta oscillations while depressing sharp-wave ripple episodes in the dorsal hippocampus.

findings.claim: This may be achieved through the enhancement of local inhibitory inputs, particularly those of somatostatin (SST)-expressing interneurons.

findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Recapitulating this transient activation of SST interneurons using chemogenetics similarly enhanced V1 plasticity.

findings.claim: Here we investigated the interactions between cholinergic and low-threshold spike (LTS) interneurons.

findings.claim: Nicotinic acetylcholine receptors (nAChRs) modulate the cholinergic drive to a hierarchy of inhibitory neurons in the superficial layers of the PFC, critical to cognitive processes.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Nicotinic acetylcholine receptors (nAChRs) modulate the cholinergic drive to a hierarchy of inhibitory neurons in the superficial layers of the PFC, critical to cognitive processes.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: In addition, recent data indicate that a genetic mutation of the α5 nAChR subunit, located on vasoactive intestinal polypeptide (VIP) inhibitory neurons, the rs16969968 single nucleotide polymorphism (α5 SNP), plays a key role in the hypofrontality observed in schizophrenia patients carrying the SNP.

findings.claim: This population of projecting GABAergic neurons comprises both somatostatin-positive (SOM<sup>+</sup>) and parvalbumin-positive (PV<sup>+</sup>) neurons that target direct and indirect pathway striatal output neurons as well as cholinergic interneurons differentially.

findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Notably, optogenetic stimulation of M1 PV<sup>+</sup> and M2 SOM<sup>+</sup> projecting neurons reduced locomotion, whereas stimulation of M1 SOM<sup>+</sup> projecting neurons enhanced locomotion.

findings.claim: We find that locomotion preferentially increases gain in pyramidal cells (PCs) that sensitize during visual stimulation compared to those that depress.
findings.claim_source_sentence: We find that locomotion preferentially increases gain in pyramidal cells (PCs) that sensitize during visual stimulation compared to those that depress.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: A model explains this differential modulation through three mechanisms: (i) variations in the strength of PV and SST connectivity to individual PCs, (ii) broad locomotion-dependent weakening of PC and PV synapses, and (iii) reduced SST inhibition targeting sensitizing PCs.

findings.claim: In previous work, we have shown that locomotion dramatically enhances neural activity in the visual cortex (V1) of the mouse (Niell and Stryker, 2010), identified the cortical circuit responsible for this enhancement (Fu et al., 2014), and shown that locomotion also dramatically enhances adult plasticity (Kaneko and Stryker, 2014).

findings.claim: We present an enhancer-AAV toolbox for accessing and perturbing striatal cell types and circuits.

findings.claim: Here, we used a Cre-dependent DREADD-chemogenetic approach to determine if inhibition of GABA interneurons in the mPFC of male mice is sufficient to produce antidepressant actions, and conversely if activation of these interneurons blocks the rapid and sustained antidepressant effects of scopolamine, a nonselective acetylcholine muscarinic receptor antagonist.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Here, we used a Cre-dependent DREADD-chemogenetic approach to determine if inhibition of GABA interneurons in the mPFC of male mice is sufficient to produce antidepressant actions, and conversely if activation of these interneurons blocks the rapid and sustained antidepressant effects of scopolamine, a nonselective acetylcholine muscarinic receptor antagonist.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Chemogenetic inhibition of all GABA interneurons (Gad1+), as well as Sst+ and Pvalb+ subtypes in the mPFC produced dose and time-dependent antidepressant effects in the forced swim and novelty suppressed feeding tests, and increased synaptic plasticity.

findings.claim: We recorded VIP-INs in brain slices from Scn1a+/-mice and wild-type littermate controls and found prominent impairment of irregular spiking (IS), but not continuous adapting (CA) VIP-INs, in Scn1a+/- mice.

findings.claim: Nicotine activates nicotinic acetylcholine receptors and improves cognitive and sensory function, in part by its actions in cortical regions.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Nicotine activates nicotinic acetylcholine receptors and improves cognitive and sensory function, in part by its actions in cortical regions.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Nicotine also enhanced the frequency of spontaneous inhibitory postsynaptic currents (sIPSCs) in Pyr, VIP, and SOM, but not PV, cells.

findings.claim: Cortical inhibitory neurons exhibit remarkable diversity in their morphology, connectivity, and synaptic properties.

findings.claim:
The range of synaptic function in the presence of inhibitory and excitatory activity within the neocortical circuit is unknown.

Results

Paired-cell recordings from pyramidal neurons in acute brain slices of mouse somatosensory cortex show that excitatory synaptic transmission is markedly suppressed during spontaneous network activity: EPSP amplitudes are 2-fold smaller and failure rates are greater than 50%.

Results

findings.effect_size_source_sentence: In fact, developmental ethanol exposure can produce long-lasting impairments in sleep, including increased sleep fragmentation and decreased delta wave amplitude.

findings.claim: In this issue of Neuron, Hendricks et al.<sup>1</sup> reveal that co-tuned pyramidal cells selectively engage somatostatin interneurons (SSTs) via like-to-like excitation, switching cortical circuits into a suppressed state that supports orientation-based figure-ground segmentation.

findings.claim: Recent studies suggest that the mGlu<sub>1</sub> subtype of metabotropic glutamate receptor regulates cortical inhibition; however, understanding the mechanisms through which mGlu<sub>1</sub> positive allosteric modulators (PAMs) regulate PFC microcircuit function and cognition is essential for advancing these potential therapeutics toward the clinic.

findings.claim: Then, via electrophysiological recordings, we showed that the spontaneous inhibitory postsynaptic current (sIPSC) frequency in pyramidal neurons increased during the neuropathic pain state and that this change was attenuated by applying a mGluR5 antagonist.

findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Indeed, synchrony between these neurons dynamically predicts approach-avoidance decisions, and optogenetically shifting the phase of synchronization by just 25 ms is sufficient to bidirectionally modulate anxiety-related behaviors.

findings.effect_size_source_sentence: We found that PV interneurons preferentially promote the synchronization of spike times when instantaneous firing rates are low (<12 Hz), whereas SST interneurons preferentially promote the synchronization of spike times when instantaneous firing rates are high (>12 Hz).

findings.effect_size_source_sentence:
Immunohistochemistry quantified interneuron density, while slice electrophysiology and optogenetics assessed inhibitory postsynaptic current (IPSC) amplitude and kinetics, quantal GABA release, and PV+- and SST-driven gamma-band oscillations (GBOs).

Results

Grin2a KO and HET mice exhibited increased PV+ and SST+ interneuron density and a shift in excitatory-inhibitory (E/I) balance favoring inhibition.

findings.claim: Over 4 decades of research support the link between Alzheimer disease (AD) and somatostatin [somatotropin-releasing inhibitory factor (SRIF)].
findings.claim_source_sentence: Over 4 decades of research support the link between Alzheimer disease (AD) and somatostatin [somatotropin-releasing inhibitory factor (SRIF)].

findings.claim: The activity of inhibitory interneurons has a profound role in shaping cortical plasticity.

findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Using chemogenetic manipulation, the activity of barrel cortex SOM-INs was blocked or activated by continuous clozapine N-oxide (CNO) administration during one-week-long deprivation.

findings.claim: Inhibitory interneurons make up around 10-20% of the total neuron population in the cerebral cortex.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Inhibitory interneurons make up around 10-20% of the total neuron population in the cerebral cortex.

findings.claim: Inhibitory interneurons orchestrate prefrontal cortex (PFC) activity, but we have a limited understanding of the molecular and experience-dependent mechanisms that regulate synaptic plasticity across PFC microcircuits.

findings.claim: We found that horizontal projections suppress superficial layers while simultaneously activating deeper cortical output layers.
findings.claim_source_sentence: We found that horizontal projections suppress superficial layers while simultaneously activating deeper cortical output layers.

findings.claim: Cortical inhibitory neurons contact each other to form a network of inhibitory synaptic connections.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Cortical inhibitory neurons contact each other to form a network of inhibitory synaptic connections.

findings.claim: Here we show that optogenetic activation of parvalbumin-positive (PV+) interneurons in the mouse primary visual cortex (V1) sharpens neuronal feature selectivity and improves perceptual discrimination.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Here we show that optogenetic activation of parvalbumin-positive (PV+) interneurons in the mouse primary visual cortex (V1) sharpens neuronal feature selectivity and improves perceptual discrimination.
findings.effect_size_source_sentence: Using multichannel recording with silicon probes and channelrhodopsin-2 (ChR2)-mediated optical activation, we found that increased spiking of PV+ interneurons markedly sharpened orientation tuning and enhanced direction selectivity of nearby neurons.

findings.claim: The connectivity diagram of neocortical circuits is still unknown, and there are conflicting data as to whether cortical neurons are wired specifically or not.
findings.claim_source_sentence: The connectivity diagram of neocortical circuits is still unknown, and there are conflicting data as to whether cortical neurons are wired specifically or not.

findings.claim: To link identified neuron types with network and behavioural functions, we recorded from the two largest genetically defined inhibitory interneuron classes, the perisomatically targeting parvalbumin (PV) and the dendritically targeting somatostatin (SOM) neurons in anterior cingulate cortex of mice performing a reward foraging task.
findings.claim_source_sentence: To link identified neuron types with network and behavioural functions, we recorded from the two largest genetically defined inhibitory interneuron classes, the perisomatically targeting parvalbumin (PV) and the dendritically targeting somatostatin (SOM) neurons in anterior cingulate cortex of mice performing a reward foraging task.

findings.claim: By selectively reducing responses to frequently, but not rarely, occurring sounds, auditory cortical neurons are thought to enhance the brain's ability to detect unexpected events through stimulus-specific adaptation (SSA).
findings.claim_source_sentence: By selectively reducing responses to frequently, but not rarely, occurring sounds, auditory cortical neurons are thought to enhance the brain's ability to detect unexpected events through stimulus-specific adaptation (SSA).

findings.claim: Deciphering the circuitry of the neocortex requires knowledge of its components, making a systematic classification of neocortical neurons necessary.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Deciphering the circuitry of the neocortex requires knowledge of its components, making a systematic classification of neocortical neurons necessary.

findings.claim: Cortical networks are composed of glutamatergic excitatory projection neurons and local GABAergic inhibitory interneurons that gate signal flow and sculpt network dynamics.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Cortical networks are composed of glutamatergic excitatory projection neurons and local GABAergic inhibitory interneurons that gate signal flow and sculpt network dynamics.

findings.claim: The neocortex contains a multitude of cell types that are segregated into layers and functionally distinct areas.
findings.claim_source_sentence: The neocortex contains a multitude of cell types that are segregated into layers and functionally distinct areas.

findings.claim: Understanding the organizational logic of neural circuits requires deciphering the biological basis of neuronal diversity and identity, but there is no consensus on how neuron types should be defined.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Understanding the organizational logic of neural circuits requires deciphering the biological basis of neuronal diversity and identity, but there is no consensus on how neuron types should be defined.

conflicts.topic: SST interneurons and surround suppression: necessary vs. sufficient
conflicts.paper_a_claim: SST-mediated lateral inhibition drives surround suppression through horizontal cortical circuits
conflicts.paper_b_claim: VIP interneurons, not SST alone, regulate cortical size tuning and visual perception, suggesting surround suppression involves VIP-SST circuit interactions
conflicts.nature_of_conflict: Whether SST interneurons are the primary mediators of surround suppression or whether VIP-SST circuit interactions are required; the VIP cell contribution complicates the SST-centric narrative
conflicts.resolution_notes: Multiple labs have shown SST involvement but the specific circuit mechanism (direct SST lateral inhibition vs. VIP→SST disinhibition) remains debated

conflicts.topic: SST = dendritic inhibition narrative vs non-Martinotti SST cells
conflicts.paper_a_claim: SST interneurons primarily target distal dendrites of pyramidal neurons, mediating dendritic inhibition
conflicts.paper_b_claim: Layer 4 SST interneurons preferentially target fast-spiking interneurons rather than pyramidal cells, and their axons remain within layer 4 rather than ascending to layer 1
conflicts.nature_of_conflict: The simplified SST=dendritic inhibition narrative breaks down in layer 4 where SST cells target somata of FS interneurons instead of pyramidal dendrites
conflicts.resolution_notes: Non-Martinotti SST cells (up to 40% of L5 SST population per Munoz et al 2017) create a fundamental problem for the blanket SST=dendritic inhibition characterization

conflicts.paper_a_claim: SOM interneurons show dense, nonspecific inhibitory connectivity with nearby pyramidal neurons
conflicts.paper_b_claim: SST subtypes possess selective connectivity with intratelencephalic or pyramidal tract neurons; synaptic targeting is selective for particular dendritic compartments
conflicts.nature_of_conflict: Whether SST interneurons connect nonspecifically (blanket inhibition) or show cell-type and compartment-specific targeting
conflicts.resolution_notes: The apparent nonspecificity in Fino & Yuste 2011 may reflect lumping of SST subtypes; when subtypes are distinguished genetically, connectivity patterns are selective

conflicts.topic: Cholinergic effects on SST: nicotinic vs muscarinic receptors
conflicts.paper_a_claim: Endogenous acetylcholine release preferentially activates nicotinic, not muscarinic receptors at Pyr-SST synapses, via presynaptic PKA signaling
conflicts.paper_b_claim: Cholinergic modulation of SST neurons drives cortical desynchronization through direct muscarinic/nicotinic activation of SOM neurons
conflicts.nature_of_conflict: Whether the dominant cholinergic mechanism is presynaptic nicotinic enhancement of Pyr→SST synapses or postsynaptic muscarinic/nicotinic depolarization of SST cells
conflicts.resolution_notes: Both mechanisms likely coexist; the Urban-Ciecko finding that endogenous ACh preferentially activates nicotinic receptors is notable because many earlier studies used bath-applied agonists

conflicts.topic: SST subtypes: discrete categories or continuous distribution
conflicts.paper_a_claim: Three discrete morphological SST subtypes in L5 (T-shaped Martinotti ~10%, fanning-out Martinotti ~50%, non-Martinotti ~40%) correlate with distinct electrophysiology
conflicts.paper_b_claim: Quantitative cluster analysis of SST interneurons reveals three electrophysiological groups but with substantial overlap in properties
conflicts.nature_of_conflict: Whether SST subtypes form genuinely discrete categories or represent peaks in a continuum of properties
conflicts.resolution_notes: Transcriptomic studies (Tasic 2018, Paul 2017) support discrete molecular subtypes, but morpho-electric properties show more continuous variation

conflicts.topic: SST activity during locomotion: suppression vs enhancement
conflicts.paper_a_claim: SST interneurons are selectively activated by cholinergic drive during active states
conflicts.paper_b_claim: Behavioral state and stimulus strength regulate the role of SST interneurons; the net effect depends on specific experimental conditions
conflicts.nature_of_conflict: Whether SST cells are uniformly activated or show context-dependent responses during locomotion and arousal states
conflicts.resolution_notes: The paradoxical suppression of some SST cells during locomotion despite cholinergic drive suggests network-level effects override direct neuromodulation

evidence_gaps.topic: SST subtype function in non-V1 cortical areas
evidence_gaps.description: Most SST subtype characterization comes from barrel cortex (Bhatt lab) and V1. Whether the same subtypes exist with same proportions and functions in prefrontal, auditory, and motor cortex is largely unknown.

evidence_gaps.topic: SST surround suppression in awake behaving animals
evidence_gaps.description: Most SST surround suppression studies used anesthetized animals or head-fixed passive viewing. The contribution of SST cells to surround suppression during active behavior and natural vision is understudied.
Nearly all SST subtype characterization is in mouse. Whether the Martinotti/non-Martinotti distinction and their circuit motifs are conserved in primates is unknown.

evidence_gaps.topic: Non-Martinotti SST cell functions in vivo
evidence_gaps.description: While Martinotti cells have clear in vivo correlates (surround suppression, dendritic gating), the in vivo function of non-Martinotti SST cells (quasi-fast-spiking, L4-targeting) remains almost entirely uncharacterized.

evidence_gaps.topic: Developmental trajectory of SST subtype specification
evidence_gaps.description: When during development SST subtypes diverge functionally and whether adult subtype identity is fixed or can be modified by experience.

evidence_gaps.topic: SST long-range projection neurons vs local interneurons
evidence_gaps.description: SST-expressing long-range projection neurons have been described but their relationship to local SST interneuron subtypes and their functional role is unclear.

unreplicated_claims.claim: SST interneurons are the primary mediators of surround suppression in V1
unreplicated_claims.notes: Multiple labs show SST involvement in size tuning but the specific contribution vs VIP and other mechanisms varies across preparations and stimulus conditions

unreplicated_claims.claim: T-shaped Martinotti cells represent ~10% of L5 SST interneurons
unreplicated_claims.notes: Specific proportions from Bhatt lab (barrel cortex); unclear if same proportions hold in other cortical areas

unreplicated_claims.claim: Pyr-to-SST synapses have ~80% failure rate in physiological calcium
unreplicated_claims.notes: n=90 pairs in Urban-Ciecko et al; failure rate is higher than previous estimates which used higher calcium concentrations

unreplicated_claims.claim: Cholinergic sensitivity of SST cells is developmentally restricted to critical period
unreplicated_claims.notes: Bhatt lab finding in V1; replication in other areas/ages needed

unreplicated_claims.claim: Non-Martinotti SST cells in L4 create disinhibitory circuit targeting FS interneurons
unreplicated_claims.notes: Confirmed in subsequent Bhatt lab papers; independent replication in other areas needed
